channel.title: Portrait & Aftermath on Shipwreck, the Rescue -- JMW Turner
channel.description: Recent content in Portrait & Aftermath on Shipwreck, the Rescue -- JMW Turner

item.title: Chapter 32: Portrait of a Hero
item.description:
Turner’s self-portrait, c.1798 — compare with figure in rescue boat

Great wooden vessel bears roughest of seas, Tossed by the beckoning deep; While souls near-free from mortal distress, Still fight till their last precious breath.

Might that be JMW’s own self-portrait stabilizing the stern of my Rescue boat? The red waistcoat. The cravat. The frontal pose. Turner was an artist seldom seen without his cravat.

item.title: Chapter 33: Steamy Chat with Christie's
item.description:
![Detail: brushwork in the rescue boat](/images/swr crop brushwork boat 3 web.jpg)

Detail: brushwork in the rescue boat

“When a letter from your girlfriend is dropped through the door, you don’t have to open it and read the name to know who it’s from.” Lawrence Gowing’s theory of the magic eye.

But Friedlander had countered: coldly analytical scholars make fewer mistakes. Martin was forced to claim the superiority of his own connoisseurship.

item.title: Chapter 34: Just Fears
item.description:
The signature on the painting

“Private collecting by connoisseurs provides for scholarship outside the strictures of officialdom.” Bob’s journal.

Independent study should hold sway over dogmatic policies of a status quo that constricts the lust for knowledge and eventually conceals the truth.

The art world’s just fears—of litigation, of reputation loss, of being wrong—drove experts to condemn rather than confirm.

This is an excerpt. Read the full chapter in Art World’s Dirty Little Secret.

item.title: Chapter 35: Tribal Instincts
item.description:
![Turner’s house in Chelsea — where he died](/images/turner house, chelsea web.jpg)

Turner’s house in Chelsea — where he died

The gentle Amiel, brooding upon his terrace at Geneva, came to more just conclusions about Wagner than did the professional litigants battling nearer Bayreuth.

Tribal instincts. The art world protects itself—not the art. Institutional self-preservation takes priority over truth.

When the stakes are greatest, the tribal response is strongest. And for Turner—some of the greatest stakes in art.

item.title: Chapter 36: A Juggler's Paradise
item.description:
![Shipwreck, the Rescue — the full story](/images/swrfullbook weba.jpg)

Shipwreck, the Rescue — the full story

In the case of second-tier artworks, quasi-monopolistic control is orchestrated by less influential groups. Seldom does one individual have the resources to control higher-priced artists independently.

A Juggler’s Paradise. The art world as marketplace—where truth, beauty, and money collide.

Bob and Graham had pierced the veil. They had seen the dirty little secret. The question was: would anyone listen?
